behavior_summary: U8P_SPIDER::look is the object's examine/U8P_SPIDER::look handler: when the player inspects the spider it emits a short bark text ('spider ') and then the process suspends.

U8P_SPIDER::gotHit handles incoming hits for the spider. It accepts two parameters (uword, word), performs an early numeric threshold check and a liveliness check (Npc::isDead), and—if conditions pass—may forward partial damage to a referenced item via Item::receiveHit.

Item damage is conditional: the script appears to forward damage only when the referenced item meets a weight threshold (>3) or matches a special id (local1 == 1). When forwarded, the damage amount is roughly half the incoming damage plus a small urandom(8) modifier and a fixed damage category (0x04).
notable_control_flow: U8P_SPIDER::look is a simple, linear process: bark 'spider ' then suspend; minimal branching.

U8P_SPIDER::gotHit contains conditional guards: an initial numeric test, a check that the NPC is not dead (Npc::isDead), and then a conditional branch that tests the referenced item (weight > 3 or id == 1) before calling Item::receiveHit.

Damage forwarding uses computed arguments (param2/2 and urandom(8)) and passes the spider context (this) to Item::receiveHit; the function contains low-confidence remnants suggesting additional minor branches or no-op jumps, but core guards above determine behavior.
open_questions: What is the precise meaning of the initial numeric threshold test referenced in U8P_SPIDER::gotHit (raw: 'this > 0x0100h') and when does it short-circuit hit processing?
Is param1 always an item handle/reference, or can it encode other kinds of IDs? The code treats it like an item for Item::* calls but this is inferred.
What is the semantics of the special-case check local1 == 1 (is '1' a sentinel item id with special behavior)?
Are there additional engine-level callers for U8P_SPIDER::gotHit beyond standard damage dispatch (this helper is a process-type utility and may be invoked by the engine or other scripts)?

main_function.name: U8P_SPIDER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspection/description text for the spider entity when the player uses 'U8P_SPIDER::look' or a U8P_SPIDER::look-type trigger; play the bark 'spider ' and then suspend.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player examines or 'looks at' the spider (process type 0x0208).
May also be triggered by fast-area/area-entry style U8P_SPIDER::look hooks consistent with object U8P_SPIDER::look handlers.

function_map.name: U8P_SPIDER::gotHit
function_map.role: hit/damage handler for the spider (interprets params, guards, may forward damage to an associated item)

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: external API: check NPC liveliness to guard hit handling

function_map.name: Item::getWeightIncludingContents
function_map.role: external API: query item weight (used to decide if item should take damage)

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: external API: apply damage to an item (used to forward partial damage)

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: external utility: generate a small random number used in damage calculation

external_calls.purpose: Guard: check whether the spider NPC is dead and skip further hit handling if so.
Decision data: obtain the referenced item's weight to decide if the item should receive forwarded damage (threshold appears to be >3).
Action: forward partial damage to the referenced item using category 0x04, amount param2/2, a urandom(8) modifier, and spider context (this).
Generate small random modifier passed into Item::receiveHit.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SPIDER::look is the object's examine/U8P_SPIDER::look handler: when the player inspects the spider it emits a short bark text ('spider ') and then the process suspends.
